metadata.title:
No MONEY FOR


IDAHO TRIAL

STATE AUDITOR REFUSES TO
PAY PETTiBONE PROSECU-
TON BILLS UNLESS SU-
PREME COURT FORCES HIM
TO.

pages.raw_text:
BOISE, Dec. 20.-State Auditor
Bragaw has complicated the Petit
bone trial situation by refusing to
honor any more deficiency warrants
to cover the expense until COM
manded to by the state supreme
court. The friction grows out of a
difference of opinion as to the wis
dom of proceeding with the re-
naming trials after the state had
been put to such heavy expense in
the Haywood case The state board
of examiners, composed of Gov.
Gooding, Attorney General Guheen
and Secretary of State LansdOn, is
determined to prosecute the cases
regardless of cost. A majority OF
the republicans and the Steunen-
berg wing of the democrats are
backing the board.


Bragaw reluctantly issued war
rants to cover the expense of the
Adams trial, but refused to honor
the Pettibone warrants and an
open rupture ensued. Gooding gave
Bragaw five minutes to decide to
issue the warrants and Bragaw re-
piled that he had already decided.
The warrants have not yet been
issued.


The state appropriation for the
cases has been exhausted and the
prosecution is left in the embarrass
ing position of being without means
to proceed. Judge Wood has held
the warrants legal, but the auditor
says nothing but a mandatory or
der from the supreme court will
cause him to issue any more OF
them
